raw.objective: “to determine the population clearance values of CBZ in epileptic patients living in Singapore”
raw.covariates: Tested:Age, WT, gender, race, formulation, comedication of phenytoin, phenobarbitone, nonanticonvulsants on CL Included: Age, WT, Phenobarbitone comedication on CL
raw.observations: Formulation differences did not show sufficient impact on serum concentrations. No impact of dose on CL
raw.equations: CL/F=theta1*(Age**theta2)*(WT**theta3)*(theta4**PHB)*exp(eta1) Css=Rij/CLj * exp(eps1) Css= steady state concentration (in this case all observed concentrations) PHB=comedication with phenobarbital (yes =1, no=0) Rij=’i’-th body weight adjusted dosing rate of CBZ (mg/kg/day) in ‘j’-th patient CLj=CL of the ’j’-th patient
raw.parameter_values: theta1=40.7 theta2=0.494 theta3=-1.17 theta4=1.44 omega1=0.0458 sigma1=0.0331

extraction_target.title: Population pharmacokinetics of carbamazepine in Singapore epileptic patients.

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

sigma.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale SIGMA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

extraction_target.residual_error: CL/F=theta1*(Age**theta2)*(WT**theta3)*(theta4**PHB)*exp(eta1) Css=Rij/CLj * exp(eps1) Css= steady state concentration (in this case all observed concentrations) PHB=comedication with phenobarbital (yes =1, no=0) Rij=’i’-th body weight adjusted dosing rate of CBZ (mg/kg/day) in ‘j’-th patient CLj=CL of the ’j’-th patient
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.objective: “to determine the population clearance values of CBZ in epileptic patients living in Singapore”

covariates.raw: Tested:Age, WT, gender, race, formulation, comedication of phenytoin, phenobarbitone, nonanticonvulsants on CL Included: Age, WT, Phenobarbitone comedication on CL

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
